meta.definition_of_concrete: A statement is concrete when an implementer can write code and tests from it without making assumptions about schemas, mappings, failure modes, or verification artifacts.

dedup.strategy: Cluster when a location overlaps and signature text is similar.

issues.title: Codex fork flow lacks a concrete add-dir transport or rejection contract
issues.subject: Codex fork flow lacks a concrete add-dir transport or rejection contract

locations.excerpt: Optional (subset used/pinned here): cwd, approvalPolicy, sandbox, persistExtendedHistory.

sources.title: Codex fork flow lacks a concrete add-dir transport or rejection contract
sources.subject: Codex fork flow lacks a concrete add-dir transport or rejection contract
sources.summary: The canonical Codex fork transport does not define any request field that can carry the accepted add-dir set, yet the universal extension spec requires fork flows to honor that set or fail safely. Without a pinned transport field or pinned rejection boundary, implementers must guess how Codex fork is supposed to satisfy add_dirs.

raw.title: Codex fork flow lacks a concrete add-dir transport or rejection contract
raw.excerpt: Optional (subset used/pinned here): cwd, approvalPolicy, sandbox, persistExtendedHistory.
raw.problem: The canonical Codex fork transport does not define any request field that can carry the accepted add-dir set, yet the universal extension spec requires fork flows to honor that set or fail safely. Without a pinned transport field or pinned rejection boundary, implementers must guess how Codex fork is supposed to satisfy add_dirs.
required_to_be_concrete: Specify the exact Codex fork/turn request field or transport mechanism that carries accepted add_dirs, or explicitly state that Codex fork rejects add_dirs before the run handle is returned.
If rejection is the intended behavior, pin the exact layer that raises the error and whether it is pre-spawn UnsupportedCapability, pre-spawn InvalidRequest, or post-acceptance Backend.
State whether turn/start, thread/fork, or both must receive the effective add-dir set when fork support exists.
Align the Codex transport contract with the universal add_dirs fork requirement so tests can target one concrete truth.
raw.notes: This is the highest-risk concrete gap because it blocks a deterministic implementation and conformance test plan for Codex fork parity.

issues.title: api — Codex fork transport for agent_api.exec.add_dirs.v1
issues.subject: Codex fork transport for agent_api.exec.add_dirs.v1

locations.excerpt: thread/fork params pin only threadId plus optional cwd, approvalPolicy, sandbox, and persistExtendedHistory.
Backends MUST honor the same effective add-dir set for new-session, resume, and forked runs, and MUST NOT silently ignore an accepted add-dir request for session-based flows.

sources.title: api — Codex fork transport for agent_api.exec.add_dirs.v1
sources.subject: Codex fork transport for agent_api.exec.add_dirs.v1
sources.summary: The universal extension contract says accepted add-dir inputs remain valid for fork flows, but the canonical Codex fork transport contract does not expose any request field that can carry those directories into thread/fork or the follow-up turn/start. As written, the Codex backend cannot both accept add_dirs for fork and satisfy the pinned transport contract.

statement_a.excerpt: Backends MUST honor the same effective add-dir set for new-session, resume, and forked runs, and MUST NOT silently ignore an accepted add-dir request for session-based flows.

statement_b.excerpt: thread/fork params pin only threadId plus optional cwd, approvalPolicy, sandbox, and persistExtendedHistory.

analysis.why_conflict: The universal extension contract says accepted add-dir inputs remain valid for fork flows, but the canonical Codex fork transport contract does not expose any request field that can carry those directories into thread/fork or the follow-up turn/start. As written, the Codex backend cannot both accept add_dirs for fork and satisfy the pinned transport contract.
possible_reconciliations: Add an explicit add-dir transport field to the Codex app-server fork/turn contract.
Or scope Codex fork out by pinning an explicit safe rejection path before the backend advertises or accepts the capability for forked runs.
what_must_be_true: The canon must define exactly how accepted add_dirs reach Codex fork flows, or exactly where Codex rejects the request instead of silently dropping it.
The planning pack, ADR, and backend-owned Codex transport docs must all say the same fork behavior.

issues.title: traceability_gap — Canonical backend mapping docs for add_dirs
issues.subject: Canonical backend mapping docs for add_dirs

locations.excerpt: Map the normalized directories into both built-in backends
Touch surface lists only Codex code files and crates/codex/src/builder/mod.rs
Touch surface lists only Claude code files and crates/claude_code/src/commands/print.rs
Resume/fork subsequences are pinned, but no add-dir mapping appears anywhere in the backend-owned Claude contract.
Codex fork/turn transport pins cwd and policy fields only; no add-dir field is described.

sources.title: traceability_gap — Canonical backend mapping docs for add_dirs
sources.subject: Canonical backend mapping docs for add_dirs
sources.summary: The pack assigns backend mapping work to code seams but does not thread those changes into the canonical backend-owned contracts that currently define Claude resume/fork argv and Codex app-server fork transport. That leaves the implementation plan disconnected from the docs that downstream readers rely on to know what the backends actually do.

raw.subject: Canonical backend mapping docs for add_dirs

locations.excerpt: Map the normalized directories into both built-in backends
Touch surface lists only Codex code files and crates/codex/src/builder/mod.rs
Touch surface lists only Claude code files and crates/claude_code/src/commands/print.rs
Resume/fork subsequences are pinned, but no add-dir mapping appears anywhere in the backend-owned Claude contract.
Codex fork/turn transport pins cwd and policy fields only; no add-dir field is described.

analysis.what_breaks: The pack assigns backend mapping work to code seams but does not thread those changes into the canonical backend-owned contracts that currently define Claude resume/fork argv and Codex app-server fork transport. That leaves the implementation plan disconnected from the docs that downstream readers rely on to know what the backends actually do.
missing_links: SEAM-3 does not include the Codex contract doc that must explain fork-path add-dir behavior or safe rejection.
SEAM-4 does not include the Claude backend mapping contract that must pin argv placement when add_dirs is present.
No seam states which canonical backend docs will be updated alongside the code changes.
required_to_be_cohesive: Extend the backend seams to name the canonical backend mapping docs they must update.
State which doc owns Codex fork add-dir transport or rejection semantics and which doc owns Claude resume/fork argv placement.
Make the acceptance path require doc/code alignment for those backend contracts before the feature is considered done.

issues.title: Claude resume/fork argv contract does not pin where add-dir appears
issues.subject: Claude resume/fork argv contract does not pin where add-dir appears

locations.excerpt: Resume/fork mappings pin ordered subsequences for --continue, --fork-session, --resume, --verbose, and final PROMPT, but never mention --add-dir.

sources.title: Claude resume/fork argv contract does not pin where add-dir appears
sources.subject: Claude resume/fork argv contract does not pin where add-dir appears
sources.summary: The plan requires Claude Code to map accepted add_dirs into one variadic --add-dir group for new, resume, and fork flows, but the canonical Claude session contract never states where that group must appear relative to the pinned session flags and the final prompt token. Deterministic argv tests therefore still require implementer guesswork.

raw.title: Claude resume/fork argv contract does not pin where add-dir appears
raw.excerpt: Resume/fork mappings pin ordered subsequences for --continue, --fork-session, --resume, --verbose, and final PROMPT, but never mention --add-dir.
raw.problem: The plan requires Claude Code to map accepted add_dirs into one variadic --add-dir group for new, resume, and fork flows, but the canonical Claude session contract never states where that group must appear relative to the pinned session flags and the final prompt token. Deterministic argv tests therefore still require implementer guesswork.
required_to_be_concrete: Pin the exact placement of the single --add-dir <DIR...> group for selector=last and selector=id in both resume and fork flows.
State whether the add-dir group must appear before or after session-selection flags such as --continue, --resume, and --fork-session.
State the absence behavior explicitly in the Claude backend contract so tests know the canonical argv when the extension is omitted.
Update the Claude backend-owned contract examples so the add_dirs mapping is testable without inferring placement from wrapper code.

issues.title: verification_gap — Capability advertisement verification for agent_api.exec.add_dirs.v1
issues.subject: Capability advertisement verification for agent_api.exec.add_dirs.v1

locations.excerpt: both built-in backends advertise and honor the key
Targeted runs while iterating: cargo test -p agent_api ... Full gate before merge: make test, make preflight
Generated artifact: docs/specs/universal-agent-api/capability-matrix.md
## agent_api.exec ... only agent_api.exec.non_interactive is present

sources.title: verification_gap — Capability advertisement verification for agent_api.exec.add_dirs.v1
sources.subject: Capability advertisement verification for agent_api.exec.add_dirs.v1
sources.summary: The pack defines capability advertisement as a success criterion, but the verification seam never threads that requirement into the canonical generated artifact that records supported capabilities. An implementation could pass local unit tests yet leave the canonical capability inventory stale, which breaks the documentation chain from code to published support surface.

locations.excerpt: both built-in backends advertise and honor the key
Targeted runs while iterating: cargo test -p agent_api ... Full gate before merge: make test, make preflight
## agent_api.exec ... only agent_api.exec.non_interactive is present
Generated artifact: docs/specs/universal-agent-api/capability-matrix.md

analysis.what_breaks: The pack defines capability advertisement as a success criterion, but the verification seam never threads that requirement into the canonical generated artifact that records supported capabilities. An implementation could pass local unit tests yet leave the canonical capability inventory stale, which breaks the documentation chain from code to published support surface.
missing_links: No seam or task explicitly regenerates docs/specs/universal-agent-api/capability-matrix.md.
No acceptance criterion says the new capability row must appear in the generated matrix for both backends.
No verification step names the generator command that keeps the canonical artifact aligned with the backend capability set.
required_to_be_cohesive: Add a verification/task step that regenerates the capability matrix and records the expected add_dirs row for Codex and Claude Code.
Tie the matrix refresh command to SEAM-5 or WS-INT so capability advertisement is verified in the same thread as backend support.
Make the pack’s acceptance criteria refer to the canonical capability artifact, not only to backend tests.

issues.title: missing_bridge_step — Codex fork parity risk resolution
issues.subject: Codex fork parity risk resolution

locations.excerpt: Codex fork transport parity is called out as a known risk.
De-risk plan says spike the fork transport first, but that spike is not a named output, dependency, or acceptance gate.
Critical path runs straight from shared normalizer to backend mapping; no explicit fork-transport decision step exists.
Current Codex fork contract has no add-dir transport hook.

sources.title: missing_bridge_step — Codex fork parity risk resolution
sources.subject: Codex fork parity risk resolution
sources.summary: The pack already knows Codex fork parity is the highest integration risk, but the thread structure still treats SEAM-3 as a normal implementation seam rather than a decision point with an explicit handoff. That makes the critical path optimistic and hides the point where the team must decide between transport support and safe rejection.

locations.excerpt: Codex fork transport parity is called out as a known risk.
Critical path runs straight from shared normalizer to backend mapping; no explicit fork-transport decision step exists.
De-risk plan says spike the fork transport first, but that spike is not a named output, dependency, or acceptance gate.
Current Codex fork contract has no add-dir transport hook.

analysis.what_breaks: The pack already knows Codex fork parity is the highest integration risk, but the thread structure still treats SEAM-3 as a normal implementation seam rather than a decision point with an explicit handoff. That makes the critical path optimistic and hides the point where the team must decide between transport support and safe rejection.
missing_links: No prerequisite decision artifact exists between the normalizer seam and the Codex fork implementation.
No workstream owns updating the Codex transport contract if the spike shows support is possible.
No acceptance gate names the exact rejection path if support is impossible.
required_to_be_cohesive: Add an explicit bridge step or named output for the Codex fork transport decision before SEAM-3 is considered implementation-ready.
Thread the result of that decision into the backend contract docs, backend code seam, and test seam.
Update the critical path so fork parity is resolved intentionally rather than discovered late in backend implementation.

issues.title: InvalidRequest error requirements remain too loose for stable add-dir tests
issues.subject: InvalidRequest error requirements remain too loose for stable add-dir tests

locations.excerpt: InvalidRequest messages ... MUST be safe-by-default ... Backends SHOULD use stable, testable messages that identify the failing field/index.

sources.title: InvalidRequest error requirements remain too loose for stable add-dir tests
sources.subject: InvalidRequest error requirements remain too loose for stable add-dir tests
sources.summary: The pack and test seam expect stable InvalidRequest behavior, but the canonical spec only says stable/testable messages are a SHOULD and does not pin the message family, field/index format, or whether tests assert strings versus structure. Two compliant implementations could therefore emit materially different safe messages and both claim conformance.

raw.title: InvalidRequest error requirements remain too loose for stable add-dir tests
raw.excerpt: InvalidRequest messages ... MUST be safe-by-default ... Backends SHOULD use stable, testable messages that identify the failing field/index.
raw.problem: The pack and test seam expect stable InvalidRequest behavior, but the canonical spec only says stable/testable messages are a SHOULD and does not pin the message family, field/index format, or whether tests assert strings versus structure. Two compliant implementations could therefore emit materially different safe messages and both claim conformance.
required_to_be_concrete: Pin whether add_dir validation tests assert exact message strings, a finite message-id set, or structured error metadata instead of free-form text.
If message text is part of the contract, define the stable templates or the allowed message variants for each validation failure class.
Define how the failing field/index is represented so backends do not invent incompatible formats.
State whether different validation failures may share one safe generic message or must remain distinguishable.

issues.title: Capability advertisement completion criteria omit the canonical generated artifact
issues.subject: Capability advertisement completion criteria omit the canonical generated artifact

locations.excerpt: Verification only names cargo test -p agent_api, make test, and make preflight.

sources.title: Capability advertisement completion criteria omit the canonical generated artifact
sources.subject: Capability advertisement completion criteria omit the canonical generated artifact
sources.summary: The pack says done means both built-in backends advertise add_dirs, and the canonical Universal Agent API docs publish that support in the generated capability matrix. The verification plan never says whether that artifact must change, how it is regenerated, or which command proves the documentation surface matches the implementation.

raw.title: Capability advertisement completion criteria omit the canonical generated artifact
raw.excerpt: Verification only names cargo test -p agent_api, make test, and make preflight.
raw.problem: The pack says done means both built-in backends advertise add_dirs, and the canonical Universal Agent API docs publish that support in the generated capability matrix. The verification plan never says whether that artifact must change, how it is regenerated, or which command proves the documentation surface matches the implementation.
required_to_be_concrete: State whether docs/specs/universal-agent-api/capability-matrix.md must be updated in the same change that lands add_dirs support.
Name the exact command that regenerates the matrix and the expected artifact delta for this feature.
Tie the feature’s acceptance criteria to the canonical matrix row so advertisement is verified at the published surface, not only in local unit tests.
